functions.name: x_transformers.attend.selective_attn
documentation: it is a technique to allow each token to prevent itself from being attended to by future tokens
if sim_head_gate not supplied, will use the first head of the attention logits (sim in this framework)

functions.name: x_transformers.attend.sparse_topk_attn
documentation: sparse topk attention - only keep topk attn logits for softmax
optional straight through with masked out logits by setting `attn_sparse_topk_straight_through = True`

functions.name: x_transformers.attend.create_causal_mask
documentation: need a special one for onnx cpu (no support for .triu)

methods.name: x_transformers.attend.Attend.forward
documentation:
einstein notation
b - batch
h - heads
n, i, j - sequence length (base sequence length, source, target)
d - feature dimension

modules.name: x_transformers.xval
documentation:
regular transformer with discrete tokens, but continuous for number
generalizes better for arithmetic
https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.02989

functions.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.dropout_seq
documentation: structured dropout, more effective than traditional attention dropouts

class_variables.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.LayerIntermediates.hiddens
documentation: all hiddens, before the final norm (in pre-norm architecture)

class_variables.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.LayerIntermediates.last_hidden
documentation: very last hidden after all attention layers, after the final norm

classes.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.PerRowDataDependentAlibi
documentation: same as data dependent alibi from forgetting transformer, but the forgetting gates are also derived by a queries and keys with a small head dimension

methods.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.LayerNorm.__init__
documentation: bias-less layernorm has been shown to be more stable. most newer models have moved towards rmsnorm, also bias-less

classes.name: x_transformers.x_transformers.Attention
documentation: attention. it is all we need
